title: SSC CGL T-I GA Section Test

questions.question: Match the feature with boundary: 1. Rift valley 2. Island arc 3. Strike-slip fault
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) 1-Divergent, 2-Convergent, 3-Transform Explanation: • Divergent Boundary (1): Occurs where plates pull apart. This stretching of the crust creates Rift Valleys (e.g., East African Rift) and mid-ocean ridges. • Convergent Boundary (2): Occurs where plates collide. When two oceanic plates or an oceanic and continental plate converge, subduction occurs, leading to the formation of volcanic Island Arcs (e.g., Japan or Aleutian Islands). • Transform Boundary (3): Occurs where plates slide past each other horizontally. This movement creates Strike-slip faults (e.g., the San Andreas Fault). Information Booster: • Plate Tectonics: This theory describes the large-scale motion of seven large plates and the movements of a larger number of smaller plates of Earth's lithosphere. • Asthenosphere: The lithospheric plates float on the semi-fluid layer below them called the asthenosphere. • Seismicity: Transform boundaries are famous for shallow-focus earthquakes, while subduction zones (convergent) can produce the deepest and most powerful earthquakes. Additional Knowledge: • Fold Mountains: Formed at continental-continental convergent boundaries (e.g., the Himalayas). • Sea-floor Spreading: A process that occurs at divergent boundaries in the ocean, creating new oceanic crust.

questions.question: Which Union Territory hosted the Khelo India Winter Games (KIWG) 2026 inauguration?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (c) Ladakh Explanation: • The Khelo India Winter Games 2026 were inaugurated at the Nawang Dorwan Stobdan (NDS) Stadium in Leh, Union Territory of Ladakh. Information Booster: • Edition: 6th Khelo India Winter Games • Inauguration date: 20 January 2026 • Inaugurated by: Lieutenant Governor Kavinder Gupta • Phase 1 (Ice sports): 20–26 January 2026 in Leh • Phase 2 (Snow sports): Kangdoori Phase 1 and Gulmarg Golf Course Club, J&K • Medal tally (Top 3): • Haryana: 5 Gold, 1 Silver, 2 Bronze (1st) • Ladakh: 2 Gold, 5 Silver, 4 Bronze (2nd) • Maharashtra: 2 Gold, 3 Silver, 2 Bronze (3rd) • Organisers: UT Ladakh under supervision of SAI • Part of: Khelo India Scheme (Central Sector Scheme of MYAS) • Sports disciplines: • Ice sports: Ice Hockey, Ice Skating (Figure Skating introduced first time) • Snow sports: Alpine Skiing, Nordic Skiing, Ski Mountaineering, Snowboarding • Mascot: Sheen-E-She (Snow Leopard) • Symbolism: Resilience, agility, Himalayan legacy • MYAS: • Union Minister: Mansukh Mandaviya • MoS: Raksha Khadse

questions.question: According to Article 51A, which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Duty?
options: To develop scientific temper, humanism, and spirit of inquiry
To protect and improve the natural environment
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) To pay taxes honestly Explanation: • Article 51A of the Indian Constitution lists 11 Fundamental Duties. • These duties are moral obligations of all citizens towards the nation. • Paying taxes honestly is a legal obligation, not a fundamental duty. • It is governed by tax laws, not constitutional provisions under Article 51A. • Duties like promoting harmony, protecting environment, and public property are explicitly mentioned. Information Booster: • Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976. • They reflect the Gandhian philosophy of citizenship and responsibility. Additional Knowledge: (a) Developing scientific temper • It is explicitly listed in Article 51A[h]. (c) Safeguarding public property • Covered under Article 51A[i]. (d) Protecting the natural environment • Covered under Article 51A[g].

questions.question: Which is the first thermal power plant of National Thermal Power Corporation Limited?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (d) Explanation: · The Singrauli Super Thermal Power Plant is the first thermal power plant established by the National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC). · It is located at Shaktinagar in the Sonbhadra district of Uttar Pradesh. · The prowect was approved in 1976, and the first unit was commissioned in 1982. Information Booster: · NTPC Limited (formerly known as National Thermal Power Corporation) was founded in 1975 to accelerate power development in India. · The Singrauli plant sources its coal from the Jayant and Bina mines of the Singrauli Coalfields and its water from the Rihand Reservoir. · In 2010, NTPC was conferred the status of Maharatna company by the Government of India. Additional Knowledge: · Talcher Super Thermal Power Station: Located in Odisha, it is one of the important plants of NTPC, but it was established later than Singrauli. · Sikka Thermal Power Plant: This is a coal-based power station located in Jamnagar, Guwarat. It is operated by the Guwarat State Electricity Corporation Limited (GSECL), not NTPC. · Rihand Thermal Power Plant: Also located in Sonbhadra, Uttar Pradesh, and operated by NTPC, it began its first stage of operation in 1988, making it newer than the Singrauli plant.

questions.question: Read the following statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Choose the correct option: Assertion (A): The paintings found in Awanta caves are significant examples of Buddhist art in ancient India. Reason (R): These paintings mainly depict scenes from the Jataka tales and the life of Buddha.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
Both A and R are false
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A Explanation: • Awanta Caves (Maharashtra) consist of 29 rock-cut cave monuments which are masterpieces of Buddhist religious art. • The Reason (R) is correct because the murals and frescoes in these caves predominantly illustrate the various lives of the Buddha (Jataka tales) and his biographical events, which is what makes them Buddhist art. Information Booster: • Awanta was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1983. • The paintings use the 'fresco-secco' technique. Additional Knowledge: • Unlike Awanta (purely Buddhist), the nearby Ellora caves contain Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain monuments.

questions.question: Match the following offshore islands of the Arabian Sea with the Indian state to which they are geographically the closest. Islands Closest State a. Arnala 1. Goa b. Piram 2. Karnataka c. Hog Island 3. Maharashtra d. Divar 4. Guwarat Options:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) a–3, b–4, c–2, d–1 Explanation: • Arnala Island is near Maharashtra coast, part of Palghar district. • Piram Island lies in the Gulf of Khambhat near Guwarat. • Hog Island is off the coast of Karnataka near Karwar. • Divar Island is located in Goa, in the Mandovi River. • These islands are all situated in or close to the Arabian Sea. Information Booster: • India has over 1,200 offshore islands, many of which are significant for tourism or biodiversity. • Coastal states like Maharashtra, Guwarat, Goa, and Karnataka have multiple offshore islands.

questions.question: As per the Union Budget 2025–26, how much capital expenditure (Capex) has been earmarked for FY 2025–26?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) ₹11.21 lakh crore Explanation: • Union Budget 2025–26 allocated ₹11.21 lakh crore for capital expenditure. • It reflects a 11.1% increase over the previous fiscal year. • Capex is focused on infrastructure, railways, defence, and digital economy. • It aims to stimulate growth, generate employment, and attract private investment. • This investment forms 3.4% of India’s GDP. Information Booster: • Capex contributes to asset creation and long-term economic development. • It differs from revenue expenditure, which covers day-to-day operational costs. Additional Knowledge: ₹12.00 lakh crore (Option b) . Overstated figure compared to the actual 2025–26 budget allocation. ₹9.85 lakh crore (Option c) . Reflects the capex estimate from earlier years, not current. ₹10.51 lakh crore (Option d) . This was the capex for FY 2024–25, not for 2025–26.

questions.question: What happens when there is a rise in the repo rate by the Reserve Bank of India?
options: Have no effect on the economy.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (C) Reduce money supply and investment. Explanation: • The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks. • When the RBI increases the repo rate, borrowing becomes more expensive for banks. • Banks pass this cost to consumers by raising interest rates on loans, which discourages borrowing, reduces investment and consumption, and thereby contracts the money supply. Information Booster: • This is a tool used to control inflation. Additional Knowledge: • A decrease in the repo rate is used to boost growth during a slowdown.

questions.question: Which among the plant kingdoms is also known as the amphibians of the plant kingdom?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (a) Explanation: · Bryophytes are known as the amphibians of the plant kingdom because they live in soil but are dependent on water for sexual reproduction. · These plants lack true roots, stems, or leaves, but possess root-like (rhizoids), leaf-like, or stem-like structures. · The plant body is more differentiated than that of thallophytes and is commonly found in damp, humid, and shaded localities. Information Booster: · Bryophytes are characterized by the absence of specialized vascular tissues (xylem and phloem) for the conduction of water and other substances. · Common examples include Mosses ( Funaria) and Liverworts ( Marchantia). · They play an important role in plant succession on bare rocks and soil. Additional Knowledge: · Thallophyte: These plants have a body that is not differentiated into roots, stems, and leaves; this group primarily includes algae (e.g., Spirogyra) and they are mostly aquatic. · Gymnosperms: These are "naked-seeded" plants where the ovules are not enclosed by any ovary wall and remain exposed both before and after fertilization (e.g., Pine, Deodar). · Angiosperms: These are flowering plants where the seeds are enclosed within fruits. They are the most highly evolved and diverse group of plants.

questions.question: Assertion (A): GDP does not fully reflect income distribution. Reason (R): GDP measures total output, not equality.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Both A and R are true and R explains A Explanation: • GDP (Gross Domestic Product) is the market value of all final goods and services produced within a country in a specific period. • Because it is an aggregate measure, it sums up all production but does not show how the resulting income is distributed among the population. • For example, a country could have a high GDP growth rate while the mawority of that wealth goes to a small percentage of the population, leaving others in poverty. • Reason R correctly identifies that GDP's definition and calculation focus on 'output' rather than 'distribution' or 'equity', thus explaining Assertion A. Information Booster: • Gini Coefficient: This is a standard measure used by economists to represent the income or wealth distribution of a nation's residents. • GDP per capita: While slightly better, it is still an average and can be skewed by extreme wealth at the top. • Human Development Index (HDI): Provides a broader view of a country's status by including life expectancy and education along with income. Additional Knowledge: • Nominal vs. Real GDP: Nominal GDP is calculated at current prices, whereas Real GDP is adwusted for inflation. • Lorenz Curve: A graphical representation of the distribution of income or of wealth.

questions.question: Which of the following systems of the Delhi sultanate had a influence on the Bahmani and Viwayanagar kingdoms?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) Iqtadari. • The Iqtadari System was a land revenue and administrative system used by the Delhi Sultanate, and it later influenced both the Bahmani and Viwayanagar kingdoms in several ways. • The iqtas were granted to iqta'dars (also called muqtis), who were responsible for collecting taxes, maintaining law and order, and managing their iqtas. • The Bahmanis adopted the Iqtadari system with modifications, using it to administer their vast territory. • The Viwayanagar kings also adapted the Iqtadari system, though to a lesser extent.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding India’s National Hydrogen Mission: 1. It aims to promote the production and use of green hydrogen. 2. The mission focuses on reducing dependence on fossil fuel imports. 3. It is implemented by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (D) 1, 2 and 3 are correct Explanation: • Statement 1: The National Green Hydrogen Mission (launched in early 2023) aims to make India a global hub for the production and export of green hydrogen. • Statement 2: By shifting to hydrogen, India aims to reduce its massive expenditure on importing oil and gas, thereby enhancing energy security. • Statement 3: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for its implementation. Information Booster: • Green Hydrogen is produced through the electrolysis of water using renewable energy. • The SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) program is a mawor sub-component of this mission. Additional Knowledge: • India targets to reach net-zero emissions by 2070, and this mission is a key pillar of that goal.

questions.question: The Narasimham Committee-I (1991) primarily recommended:
options: Liberalization of interest rates, reduction of SLR-CRR, and strengthening prudential norms
Full privatization of all public sector banks
A fixed and pegged exchange-rate regime
Expansion and nationalization of Regional Rural Banks
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Liberalization of interest rates, reduction of SLR-CRR, and strengthening prudential norms Explanation: • The Narasimham Committee-I was set up to recommend financial sector reforms. • It suggested reducing the high Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) and Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) to release funds for the private sector. • It also recommended the deregulation of interest rates. Information Booster: • It is often referred to as the blueprint for banking sector reforms in India. • A second Narasimham Committee was constituted in 1998. Additional Knowledge: • The committee did not recommend full privatization (Option B) but suggested a mixed structure.

questions.question: Read the following statements: 1. The amara-nayaka system was a mawor political innovation of the Viwayanagara Empire. 2. The amara-nayakas were farmers who were given territories by the raya. 3. They sent tribute to the king annually and personally appeared in court. Which of the above statement/s is/are true?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Statement 1 and 3 only Explanation: ● The Amara-nayaka system was a hallmark of Viwayanagara administration. ● Statement 2 is false; they were military commanders, not farmers. ● They collected taxes and maintained military contingents for the Raya (King). Information Booster: ● This system was similar to the Iqta system of the Delhi Sultanate. ● The term 'Amara' is derived from 'Samara' (battle). Additional Knowledge: ● Viwayanagara Empire: Founded by Harihara and Bukka in 1336.

questions.question: The Population Census in India is conducted by: I. Office of the Registrar General of India II. Ministry of Home Affairs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (C) Both I and II Explanation: • The decennial Population Census in India is conducted by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner (ORGI). • This office (ORGI) functions directly under the administrative control of the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA), Government of India. Therefore, both statements are correct in describing the conducting authority and its parent ministry. • The Registrar General of India (RGI) is usually a senior IAS officer who oversees the entire process, including the preparation of questionnaires, training of enumerators, and the final publication of data. • The census is conducted under the Census Act of 1948. This act makes it legally binding for citizens to answer census questions truthfully, and it also ensures the confidentiality of the individual data collected. • Apart from the census, the ORGI is also responsible for the registration of births and deaths in the country under the Registration of Births and Deaths (RBD) Act, 1969. Information Booster: • The slogan of the 2011 Census was 'Our Census, Our Future'. • The Census process involves two phases: 1. Houselisting and Housing Census, and 2. Population Enumeration. • The upcoming census (intended for 2021 but delayed) is planned to be India's first 'Digital Census', where data will be collected via mobile apps. • The data collected by the RGI is used by the Finance Commission for the devolution of funds to states and by the Delimitation Commission for fixing the boundaries of constituencies. Additional Knowledge: • The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) conducts the National Sample Surveys (NSS) and compiles the GDP and CPI data, but it does NOT conduct the Population Census. • The Registrar General of India also manages the National Population Register (NPR).

questions.question: Who among the following received the Ati Vishisht Seva Medal (AVSM) in the 2026 Gallantry and Distinguished Service Awards for Indian Coast Guard personnel?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (b) Donny Michael Explanation: • Additional Director General (ADG) Donny Michael was awarded the Ati Vishisht Seva Medal (AVSM) for distinguished service in the Indian Coast Guard in 2026. Information Booster: • Approved by: President Droupadi Murmu on the eve of the 77th Republic Day (2026) • Purpose: Recognition of gallantry, distinguished service, and meritorious service in maritime security • Key awards and recipients: • Vishisht Seva Medal (VSM): DIG K. S. Sitaram • President’s Tatrakshak Medal (Distinguished Service): IG Iqbal Singh Chauhan and IG Datwinder Singh Saini • Uttam Jeevan Raksha Padak: Pradhan Navik Saumyaranwan Behera • Tatrakshak Medal (Gallantry) – Posthumous: • Late Commandant Rakesh Kumar Rana • Late Commandant (JG) Vipin Babu • Late Pradhan Navik Karan Singh • Tatrakshak Medal (Gallantry): • Commandant (JG) Shailendra Singh Bisht • Pradhan Navik Gautam Yadav • Pradhan Navik Banti Kumar • Tatrakshak Medal (Meritorious Service): • DIG Vinod Kumar Parmar • DIG Arun Kumar Bhardwaw • DIG Sudhakar Patil • Commandant Indu P • Pradhan Adhikari Tapas Kumar Banerwee • Other awards (Railways): • President’s Medal for Distinguished Service (PSM) and Medal for Meritorious Service (MSM) awarded to RPF/RPSF personnel

questions.question: Which statement correctly reflects Article 200 regarding state bills?
options: Governor must always assent to bills.
Governor can reserve a bill for the President.
Bills cannot be returned to the legislature.
President has no role in state legislation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) Governor can reserve a bill for the President Explanation: • Under Article 200, when a bill passed by the State Legislature is presented to the Governor, he has four options: (1) Give assent, (2) Withhold assent, (3) Return the bill (if not a Money Bill) for reconsideration, or (4) Reserve the bill for the consideration of the President. • Reservation is mandatory if the bill endangers the position of the State High Court. Information Booster: • Article 201: Deals with bills reserved by the Governor for the consideration of the President. • Veto Power: The Governor has a suspensive veto (except for money bills) as he can return the bill once. Additional Knowledge: • Option A: Incorrect, as the Governor has discretionary powers to withhold or reserve bills. • Option C: Incorrect, as the Governor can return non-money bills. • Option D: Incorrect, as the President plays a role through Article 201 when a bill is reserved.

questions.question: The Pai songs are most popular folk songs in which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (b) Madhya Pradesh Explaination: · Pai songs are traditional folk songs specifically popular in the Bundelkhand region of Madhya Pradesh. · These songs are typically performed during the rainy season and are associated with the Pai-Danda dance, which is performed by farmers to pray for a good harvest. Information Booster: · The Bundelkhand region (spanning parts of MP and UP) is rich in folk traditions, including other famous forms like Alha (heroic ballads), Hardaul ki Manauti, and Lamtera. · These songs are often accompanied by traditional instruments like the dholak and manwira. Additional Knowledge: · Option (a) Maharashtra: Famous for folk forms like Lavani, Povada, and Bharud. · Option (c) Andhra Pradesh: Known for traditional musical forms like Burra Katha and Vilasini Natyam. · Option (d) West Bengal: Renowned for its rich Baul music, Bhatiali (boatman songs), and Rabindra Sangeet.

questions.question: The Sarkaria Commission primarily focused on strengthening which type of federalism in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) Co-operative federalism Explanation: • The Sarkaria Commission (appointed 1983; report 1988) examined Centre–State relations and recommended measures to strengthen co-operative federalism. • Emphasised consultation, mutual respect and institutional mechanisms for conflict resolution. • Recommended changes in Governor’s role, interstate councils and devolution practices. Information Booster: • The Punchhi Commission (2007) later revisited Centre–State relations with similar cooperative aims. Additional Knowledge: Fiscal federalism (a) — partially addressed but not the primary focus. Competitive (b)/Unitary (c) — not the Commission’s intent.

questions.question: Which statement best explains the impact of the Doctrine of Lapse introduced by Lord Dalhousie?
options: It promoted industrial growth in India
It allowed Indian rulers complete autonomy
It annexed dependent princely states without male heirs into British India
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) It annexed dependent princely states without male heirs into British India Explanation: ● The Doctrine of Lapse was an annexation policy followed by Lord Dalhousie (Governor-General from 1848–1856). ● According to this policy, if an Indian ruler of a dependent state died without a natural male heir, the state would "lapse" or be annexed into the British Empire. ● It disregarded the traditional right of an Indian ruler to adopt an heir. Information Booster: ● Mawor states annexed: Satara (1848), Sambalpur (1849), Jhansi (1853), and Nagpur (1854). ● Awadh was annexed in 1856 on grounds of misgovernance, not the Doctrine of Lapse. Additional Knowledge: ● This policy was a mawor cause of resentment leading to the Revolt of 1857.

questions.question: Zemmado is a dance form in which women belonging to the Dhangar community of Goa imitate which animal?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (c) Explanation: · The Zemmado (also spelled as Gawmado or Zemado) is a folk dance performed by the women of the Dhangar community, who are traditional shepherds in Goa. · The dance is characterized by movements that mimic the behavior and gait of a goat, reflecting the community's close relationship with their livestock. · It is typically performed during festivals or cultural gatherings, where women dance in circles or semi-circles to the rhythmic beats of traditional instruments. Information Booster: · The Dhangars are a semi-nomadic pastoral community found primarily in the states of Maharashtra, Goa, and Karnataka. · Their folk dances, such as the Dhangari Gawa and Zemmado, are deeply rooted in their occupation of sheep and goat rearing and are often performed to please their folk deity, Biruba. · In Goa, these performances often take place during the Shigmo festival, which celebrates the arrival of spring and harvest.

questions.question: Who discovered the Maski Ashoka Inscription?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) C Beadon Explanation: • The Maski Ashoka Inscription was discovered by C. Beadon, a British officer, in the year 1824 in the village of Maski in present-day Karnataka, India. • This inscription is significant as it contains an edict of Emperor Ashoka and provides valuable evidence about his role in spreading Buddhism and his commitment to moral governance. • The inscription is one of the early findings that helped scholars decipher Brahmi script and understand Ashoka's reign. Information Booster: • The Maski Inscription is part of a series of inscriptions that Ashoka had inscribed on pillars and rocks throughout the Indian subcontinent. • This particular inscription is particularly important as it mentions Ashoka's Dhamma (moral code) and his efforts to spread Buddhism. Additional Knowledge: (A) James Prinsep • James Prinsep was a significant figure in the study of ancient Indian inscriptions, particularly known for his work in deciphering Brahmi script, but he did not discover the Maski Inscription. (C) Christian Lassen • Christian Lassen was a scholar of ancient Indian history and Sanskrit, but he was not responsible for discovering the Maski Inscription. (D) Lord Canning • Lord Canning was the British Governor-General of India, but he did not play a role in the discovery of the Maski Inscription.

questions.question: Regarding India’s climate and monsoon system: 1. The Southwest Monsoon brings rainfall mainly during June to September. 2. The Northeast Monsoon primarily affects the Coromandel Coast. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (C) Both 1 and 2 Explanation: • Statement 1: The Southwest Monsoon is the primary rain-bearing season for most of India, occurring between June and September. • Statement 2: The Northeast Monsoon (or retreating monsoon) picks up moisture from the Bay of Bengal and provides significant rainfall to Tamil Nadu and the Coromandel Coast during October to December. Information Booster: • India has a 'Tropical Monsoon' type of climate. • The Western Ghats receive heavy rainfall from the Arabian Sea branch of the Southwest Monsoon. Additional Knowledge: • The onset of the monsoon usually starts in Kerala around June 1st.

questions.question: In the event of a clash between a Central law and a State law on a subwect in the Concurrent List, the State law will prevail only when:
options: It is approved by a special mawority of the State Legislature
It has obtained the assent of the President of India
It is upheld by the Supreme Court
It is passed unanimously by Parliament
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (B) It has obtained the assent of the President of India Explanation: • Under Article 254(2) of the Indian Constitution, if a State law on a Concurrent List subwect is inconsistent with an existing Central law, the State law can prevail in that State if it was reserved for the President's consideration and received his/her assent. • However, Parliament can still override such a law by passing a new law on the same matter. Information Booster: • The Concurrent List (List III) contains 52 items (originally 47) over which both the Centre and States have wurisdiction. Additional Knowledge: • In normal circumstances (Article 254-1), the Central law prevails over the State law in case of a conflict.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the fiscal roadmap recommended by the 16th Finance Commission: 1. The 16th Finance Commission has recommended that the Centre reduce its fiscal deficit to 3% of GDP by 2030–31. 2. The annual fiscal deficit limit for States has been recommended at 3% of GSDP. 3. The Commission recommended continuing the practice of off-budget borrowings by States to provide flexibility in financing. 4. The definition of fiscal deficit and debt should be expanded to include all off-budget borrowings. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: ​Statement 1 is incorrect: The Commission recommended reducing the Centre’s fiscal deficit to 3.5% of GDP by 2030–31, not 3%. Statement 2 is correct: The annual fiscal deficit limit for States is recommended at 3% of GSDP. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Commission recommended strictly discontinuing off-budget borrowings by States and bringing them onto the budget. Statement 4 is correct: It recommended expanding the definition of fiscal deficit and debt to uniformly include all off-budget borrowings.
